content.problemStatement:
For encoding an even-length binary string into a sequence of `A`, `T`, `C`, and `G`, we iterate from **left to right** and replace the characters as follows:


`00` is replaced with `A`
`01` is replaced with `T`
`10` is replaced with `C`
`11` is replaced with `G`
Given a binary string SSS of length NNN (NNN is even), find the encoded sequence.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
First line contains a single integer NNN, the length of the sequence.
Second line contains binary string SSS of length NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the encoded sequence.


**Note:** Output is **case-sensitive**.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 1000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an even integer N, the length of the binary string; the second line contains a binary string S of length N consisting only of characters '0' and '1'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the encoded DNA sequence obtained by replacing every consecutive pair of bits according to the mapping: 00→A, 01→T, 10→C, 11→G.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each binary string is processed in pairs: 00→A, 01→T, 10→C, 11→G. The resulting DNA sequences are printed line by line.
First test case pairs: 11→G, 00→A, 11→G, 00→A, giving GAGAG. Second test case consists of five 01 pairs, each mapping to T.
Pairs are 11,00,11,00,11,00,11 → G A G A G A G, concatenated as GAGAGAG.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the binary string from left to right in steps of two characters. For each pair we look up the corresponding DNA character using a simple if‑else chain or a switch on the two‑character substring. The resulting characters are concatenated to form the answer. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) additional memory besides the output string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the binary string. Overall O(total N) ≤ O(1000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the output string of each test case; auxiliary space is O(1).
